name: drillable-context
description: Grounds your coding agent in YOUR project's facts — your docs, decisions, and conventions — so it drills them instead of guessing, and says "no record" instead of bluffing. A local MCP server over a folder of markdown you point it at.

facts_dir.title: Facts directory
facts_dir.description: A folder of markdown your agent should ground in — your docs/, notes, decisions, conventions. The .md files stay your source of truth.

oracle_repo.title: Oracle repo (optional)
oracle_repo.description: A code repo to re-check file/PR references against, so a fact citing a moved file is flagged stale. Leave blank to ground against the facts themselves.

semantic_retrieval.title: Semantic retrieval (sends facts to OpenAI to embed)
semantic_retrieval.description: Embed your facts for semantic search — recommended for a large corpus (recall@3 ~94% vs ~67% keyword). Requires OPENAI_API_KEY and sends your facts' text to OpenAI. This also turns on doc2query: an LLM predicts the everyday-worded questions each note answers and adds them to the search index only (never to what's shown), so plain-language queries still find the right note (measured: +16–25 pts recall, no precision loss). It uses the same data and vendor you've already opted into — a few cents one-time, cached. Off = fully local keyword retrieval, no OpenAI.

rerank.title: Rerank low-confidence searches (extra accuracy, per-search OpenAI cost)
rerank.description: When a search looks uncertain — usually a plain-worded question whose terms your notes don't use — have an LLM re-order the top candidates by relevance before answering (measured: everyday-question recall@5 ~78%→93%). Needs Semantic retrieval ON. Unlike that, this costs a small OpenAI call PER affected search (fires on roughly a third of searches — the unsure ones; confident searches skip it). Off = no per-search cost.
